title: The Art of Taking Smart Notes
author: Sonke Ahrens
url: https://example.com/smart-notes

Taking smart notes is a practice that transforms how you engage with information. Rather than simply highlighting passages or copying quotes, the goal is to process ideas into your own words and connect them to existing knowledge. This creates a web of understanding rather than a collection of isolated facts.

The slip-box method, popularized by sociologist Niklas Luhmann, relies on writing atomic notes that each capture a single idea. By linking these notes together, emergent patterns and insights surface naturally over time. The act of writing itself forces clarification of thought, making the system valuable even if you never revisit a note.
